task_completion.description: A threshold mask for bone tissue is selected or applied to the dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The threshold panel in InVesalius indicates a 'Bone' preset is active, or the 2D slice views show the bone structure highlighted with a mask color.

task_completion.description: A 3D surface of the skull is generated and displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D rendering of the skull is visible in the 3D viewer pane of the InVesalius interface.

task_completion.description: A sagittal cut is applied to the 3D skull surface to reveal the internal cross-section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D skull model is sliced vertically from front to back (along the sagittal plane), exposing the internal bone structure such as the cranial diploë and sinuses.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the export or saving of the visualization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Picture' or 'Save As' dialog is open over the InVesalius window, or a system screenshot tool is actively capturing the 3D viewer.

task_completion.description: The screenshot is saved to the exact requested path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the file name 'skull_cross_section.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or a file manager/terminal verifies the file's presence at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the InVesalius GUI to perform the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the InVesalius application open with tools being actively used, rather than just terminal commands or scripts generating the output.

integrity.description: The 3D model shown is genuinely generated from the provided CT Cranium dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D model matches the anatomical region of a human skull, and the InVesalius interface displays the 'CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample' patient name or dataset details.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply download or copy a pre-existing image of a skull cross-section.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the step-by-step process of generating the surface and cutting it within the InVesalius software, rather than just downloading an image from the web.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is a CT scan of a human skull ('CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample'). The 3D surface should depict a skull. A sagittal cut divides the skull into left and right halves, revealing the internal cross-section (cranial diploë and sinuses). The final screenshot must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/skull_cross_section.png'.
